none.justification: Answer A correctly identifies the USE_JEMALLOC source-file sites/subsystems, correctly treats debug.c's jemalloc DEBUG MALLCTL code as memory-allocator rather than crash reporting, and correctly places HAVE_BACKTRACE in debug.c crash/debug reporting. The real GT-scoring gap is that it omits the src/Makefile jemalloc build/link dependency effect, which the rubric explicitly says should be mentioned.

ccodegraph.justification: Answer B is badly incomplete: it restricts USE_JEMALLOC to the literal `#ifdef` spelling and lists only 4 sites, relegating most required `#if defined(USE_JEMALLOC)` files to an unexpanded note, and it also reports only 10 HAVE_BACKTRACE sites instead of the GT's 15. Its subsystem classifications are mostly right, but the requested inventory is missing too much, and it omits the Makefile jemalloc effect.

lsp.justification: Answer C makes the same strict-syntax mistake, listing only 4 USE_JEMALLOC sites and excluding the many required `#if defined(USE_JEMALLOC)` gates across Redis source files, so the main inventory is substantially incomplete. It also reports only 10 HAVE_BACKTRACE occurrences rather than all 15 and misses the Makefile jemalloc build/link effect, though its classifications for the sites it lists are correct.
